name: prime

relevance.justification: PRIME is a perfect fit for the Demographic-KG pipeline. It provides all three required dimensions: (1) per-sample demographic metadata (age, sex, BMI, race/ethnicity, country, smoking/drinking status, diet, and more), (2) species-level microbiome data (ASV-level taxonomic profiles at phylum through species from both SILVA 138.2 and Greengenes2 2024.09), and (3) rich phenotypic data across 101 disease/health phenotypes covering 93 body sites. It is actively maintained (hosted on jCloud at SJTU, continuously updated with new studies) and is already at 53,449 samples from 111 studies as of the NAR 2026 paper.

novelty.justification: PRIME is entirely novel to BioThings. No existing BioThings API (mygene.info, mychem.info, myvariant.info, mydisease.info) ingests human 16S rRNA microbiome data with harmonized per-sample phenotypic metadata. The combination of standardized taxonomic abundance tables + manually curated demographic/clinical metadata per sample is unique. The HumanMetagenomeDB (already reviewed) covers metagenomic samples; PRIME covers 16S rRNA amplicon data, a distinct and complementary scope. No overlap with any source in existing-biothings-plugins.json.

openness.justification: Fully open. The paper states 'All data in PRIME are openly accessible without login or restriction.' The API itself confirms 'No authentication required' and 'No rate limiting currently applied.' The cache download endpoint at /api/v1/abundances/cache/:db/:rank/:type returns CSV files directly with no auth. The metadata POST endpoints also require no auth. License is not explicitly named on the website but the paper and database state free, open access. Confirmed by direct API access during inspection.

evidence.scope: Curated and standardized database of human-associated 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing data. Contains 53,449 samples from 111 public studies across 93 body sites and 101 phenotypic categories. Each sample has: (1) manually curated sample-level metadata (demographics, phenotype, body site, sequencing protocol, experimental design, geographic origin), (2) taxonomic abundance tables at phylum-species for both SILVA 138.2 and Greengenes2 2024.09 as absolute and relative abundances, and (3) NCBI Taxonomy-mapped taxon identifiers.
evidence.overlap: No overlap with existing BioThings sources. PRIME is the only source covering 16S rRNA microbiome + harmonized demographic/phenotypic metadata for pending.api.

license.name: Open Access (no explicit license stated; free, unrestricted access confirmed)

api_info.rate_limits: none (paper and API both confirm no rate limiting)
api_info.record_coverage: full; bulk cache endpoints return all 53,449 samples

risks: No explicit open-source license (MIT/CC) stated — free access is confirmed but redistribution terms are not formally specified. Should be flagged in design_rationale.md.

Bulk metadata download is only via POST batch API (no single-file metadata dump like the abundance cache) — the plugin will need to paginate through sample IDs or use the samples/list endpoint to enumerate all SRR IDs, then batch-fetch metadata.
API-only ingestion required (no flat-file metadata dump) — plugin will need a custom API-crawl dumper.py for metadata; abundance data has direct cache CSV endpoints.
Database is hosted on institutional infrastructure (jCloud/SJTU) — long-term availability depends on institutional support, which the paper says is guaranteed.
next_actions: Run site inspection to confirm all cache URLs are accessible and sample metadata schema
Proceed to plugin generation with hybrid ingestion: cache CSV for abundances + API crawl for sample metadata
